message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracking database

Update beads JSONL export with latest issue status changes from
multi-agent development. Reflects progress on GPU effects,
mouse interactions, runtime enhancements, and documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.5","title":"Responsive Layout Primitives (Breakpoints + Auto UI Height)","description":"## Task
Add responsive layout primitives: breakpoints and optional auto UI height for inline mode.

## Scope
- Breakpoint model (xs/sm/md/lg) with helper selectors.
- Auto UI height measurement pass with caching.
- Ensure inline mode respects measured height.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for breakpoint matching and selector logic.
- Snapshot tests at multiple terminal sizes.
- PTY E2E: resize across breakpoints with JSONL logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Layout switches predictably at breakpoints.
- Auto height does not thrash or flicker.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:23.413089957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:40.006529721Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:40.006508431Z","close_reason":"Implemented breakpoints + inline auto UI height","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:23.413089957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:25.518152066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Terminal Capability Strategy for Tear‑Free Output
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums

- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization

 {"id":"bd-34lz","title":"Widget API E2E Test Script","description":"## Purpose

Create a comprehensive E2E test script for Widget API verification with detailed logging.

## Script: scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-/tmp/widget_api_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

echo "=== Widget API E2E Test Suite ==="
echo "Log directory: $LOG_DIR"
echo "Started at: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo ""

# Step 1: Workspace Build
echo "[1/7] Building workspace..."
cargo build --workspace 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/01_build.log"
echo "✓ Build complete"

# Step 2: Unit Tests
echo "[2/7] Running unit tests..."
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/02_test.log"
echo "✓ Tests complete"

# Step 3: Clippy
echo "[3/7] Running clippy..."
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/03_clippy.log"
echo "✓ Clippy complete"

# Step 4: Feature Combinations
echo "[4/7] Testing feature combinations..."
for feature in canvas forms charts; do
  echo "  - ftui-extras --features $feature"
  cargo check -p ftui-extras --features $feature 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/04_features.log"
done
echo "✓ Features complete"

# Step 5: Documentation Build
echo "[5/7] Building documentation..."
cargo doc --workspace --no-deps 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/05_doc.log"
echo "✓ Docs complete"

# Step 6: Widget Signature Verification
echo "[6/7] Verifying Widget signatures..."
echo "Checking for old Buffer signatures..."
if rg 'fn render.*buf: &mut Buffer' crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs 2>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: Found old Buffer signature\!"
  exit 1
fi
echo "Checking for new Frame signatures..."
rg 'fn render.*frame: &mut Frame' crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs | tee "$LOG_DIR/06_signatures.log"
echo "✓ All signatures use Frame"

# Step 7: Snapshot Tests
echo "[7/7] Running snapshot tests..."
cargo test -p ftui-harness --test widget_snapshots 2>&1 | tee "$LOG_DIR/07_snapshots.log"
echo "✓ Snapshots complete"

# Summary
echo ""
echo "=== E2E Test Suite Complete ==="
echo "Ended at: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "Logs: $LOG_DIR/"
ls -la "$LOG_DIR/"
```

## Detailed Logging

Each step writes to a separate log file:
- `01_build.log` - Full cargo build output
- `02_test.log` - All test results with RUST_LOG=debug
- `03_clippy.log` - Clippy warnings/errors
- `04_features.log` - Feature combination checks
- `05_doc.log` - Documentation build output
- `06_signatures.log` - Widget signature grep results
- `07_snapshots.log` - Snapshot test results

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Script is executable and passes on current codebase
- [ ] All logs are captured with timestamps
- [ ] Exit code 0 only if ALL steps pass
- [ ] Any failure provides clear error message and stops execution","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:37:07.529977078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:59:00.617534297Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:59:00.617511184Z","close_reason":"E2E script is functional and correctly identifies test failures. Created bd-124n for event_coalescer test failures found during E2E run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-34pj","title":"Form Validation Framework","description":"## Background

Forms screen (bd-3vbf.18) exists but validation is manual. Common needs:
- Required fields
- Email/URL format
- Min/max length
- Pattern matching
- Cross-field validation

## Solution: Declarative Validation

```rust
pub trait Validator: Send + Sync {
    fn validate(&self, value: &str) -> ValidationResult;
    fn message(&self) -> &str;
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum ValidationResult {
    Valid,
    Invalid { message: String },
    Warning { message: String },  // Valid but flagged
    Pending,  // Async validation in progress
}

// Built-in validators
pub struct Required;
pub struct MinLength(usize);
pub struct MaxLength(usize);
pub struct Pattern { regex: Regex, message: String }
pub struct Email;
pub struct Url;

// Composable
pub struct All(Vec<Box<dyn Validator>>);
pub struct Any(Vec<Box<dyn Validator>>);

// Field wrapper
pub struct ValidatedField<W> {
    widget: W,
    validators: Vec<Box<dyn Validator>>,
    result: ValidationResult,
    show_on: ShowValidation,  // OnBlur, OnChange, OnSubmit
}

impl<W: Widget> ValidatedField<W> {
    pub fn validate(&mut self) -> bool;
    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn error_message(&self) -> Option<&str>;
}

// Form-level validation
pub struct Form {
    fields: Vec<Box<dyn ValidatableField>>,
}

impl Form {
    pub fn validate_all(&mut self) -> bool;
    pub fn first_error(&self) -> Option<(usize, &str)>;
    pub fn errors(&self) -> Vec<(usize, &str)>;
}
```

## Visual Feedback

```
Email: john@example.com  ✓
Password: ****           ✗ Must be at least 8 characters
         ├────────────────┘
```

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-extras/src/validation.rs
- crates/ftui-extras/src/validators/mod.rs
- crates/ftui-extras/src/validated_field.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Form Validation Framework
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-34pj.1","title":"Validator Trait and Built-in Rules","description":"Core validation infrastructure with common validators.

## Requirements
- Validator trait with validate(value) -> Result
- Built-in: required, min_length, max_length, pattern, email, url
- Composable validators (and, or, not)
- Custom error messages with i18n support

## Proposed API

```rust
pub trait Validator<T>: Send + Sync {
    fn validate(&self, value: &T) -> ValidationResult;
    fn error_message(&self) -> &str;
}

pub enum ValidationResult {
    Valid,
    Invalid(ValidationError),
}

pub struct ValidationError {
    pub code: &'static str,      // e.g., "required", "too_short"
    pub message: String,         // Human-readable
    pub params: HashMap<String, String>,  // For interpolation
}

// Built-in validators
pub struct Required;
pub struct MinLength(pub usize);
pub struct MaxLength(pub usize);
pub struct Pattern(pub Regex);
pub struct Email;
pub struct Url;
pub struct Range<T: Ord>(pub T, pub T);

// Composition
pub struct And<A, B>(pub A, pub B);
pub struct Or<A, B>(pub A, pub B);
pub struct Not<V>(pub V);

// Builder pattern
pub fn validate<T>() -> ValidatorBuilder<T>;

impl<T> ValidatorBuilder<T> {
    pub fn required(self) -> Self;
    pub fn min_length(self, len: usize) -> Self;
    pub fn max_length(self, len: usize) -> Self;
    pub fn pattern(self, regex: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn custom(self, f: impl Fn(&T) -> ValidationResult) -> Self;
    pub fn with_message(self, msg: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn build(self) -> Box<dyn Validator<T>>;
}
```

## Error Message Interpolation

```rust
// Template: "Must be at least {min} characters"
// Params: {"min": "8"}
// Result: "Must be at least 8 characters"

impl ValidationError {
    pub fn with_params(mut self, key: &str, value: impl ToString) -> Self {
        self.params.insert(key.to_string(), value.to_string());
        self
    }
    
    pub fn format_message(&self) -> String {
        let mut result = self.message.clone();
        for (key, value) in &self.params {
            result = result.replace(&format\!("{{{}}}", key), value);
        }
        result
    }
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-extras/src/validation/validators.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_required_empty: Fails on empty string
- test_required_whitespace: Configurable whitespace handling
- test_min_length: Boundary testing (n-1, n, n+1)
- test_pattern_email: Valid and invalid emails
- test_composition_and: Both must pass
- test_composition_or: Either can pass
- test_custom_validator: User-defined logic
- test_error_interpolation: Parameter substitution

## Built-in Error Codes
| Code | Default Message |
|------|-----------------|
| required | This field is required |
| too_short | Must be at least {min} characters |
| too_long | Must be at most {max} characters |
| pattern | Invalid format |
| email | Invalid email address |
| url | Invalid URL |

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Validator Trait and Built-in Rules
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

-{"id":"bd-34pj.2","title":"Form State Manager","description":"Track form field values, touched state, and errors.

## Requirements
- Field value storage with change tracking
- Touched/dirty state per field
- Error state per field
- Form-level validity check

## Implementation
- FormState struct with field map
- set_value(), set_touched(), get_errors()
- is_valid() for form submission check

## Tests
- State transitions
- Error aggregation
- Form validity calculation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Form State Manager

- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
